differential_diagnoses: Lemierre-like syndrome secondary to duodenal-venous fistula (Streptococcus anginosus sepsis)
Penetrating duodenal ulcer causing retroperitoneal abscess and secondary venous thrombosis
Infective endocarditis (Streptococcus anginosus) with septic pulmonary emboli
Secondary antiphospholipid syndrome due to infection
Portal vein thrombosis or pylephlebitis secondary to occult intra-abdominal infection
next_steps: Perform transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) to rule out endocarditis
Perform esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) to evaluate for duodenal pathology and fistula
Continue targeted antibiotic therapy for Streptococcus anginosus based on sensitivities
Continue therapeutic anticoagulation for documented renal vein/IVC thrombosis
Consider repeat abdominal imaging (CT or MRI) once patient is stabilized and able to minimize motion artifact
Consult infectious disease, gastroenterology, and vascular surgery

differential_diagnoses: Penetrating duodenal ulcer with retroperitoneal abscess and secondary septic thrombophlebitis
Streptococcus anginosus bacteremia secondary to occult gastrointestinal pathology
Secondary antiphospholipid syndrome due to severe infection
next_steps: Perform esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) to assess the duodenal wall for penetrating ulcer or fistula
Perform transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) to definitively rule out infective endocarditis
Obtain follow-up imaging (CT or MRI) under sedation or with motion-correction protocols to characterize the retroperitoneal/duodenal connection
Check serum Strongyloides serology and perform serial stool examinations for larvae
Continue targeted antibiotic therapy for Streptococcus anginosus and reassess anticoagulation status pending EGD findings
Consult interventional radiology for possible abscess drainage if indicated by repeat imaging
